coverage_checks.rule: exact configured jurisdiction and office selectors

seattle_applicability.explanation: The target ballot geography is the City of Seattle.
The official map places most of Seattle in Congressional District 7.
The official map places part of southeast Seattle in Congressional District 9.
The official map places part of Seattle in County Council District 2.
The official map places part of Seattle in County Council District 4; it has no August primary contest.
The official map places part of Seattle in County Council District 8.
The City of Seattle is within King County.
The official map places part of southeast Seattle in Legislative District 11.
The official map places part of northwest Seattle in Legislative District 32.
The official map places part of southwest Seattle in Legislative District 34.
The official map places part of northwest Seattle in Legislative District 36.
The official map places part of southeast Seattle in Legislative District 37.
The official map places part of central Seattle in Legislative District 43.
The official map places part of north Seattle in Legislative District 46.

aliases: Metropolitan King County Council District No. 2

races.display_name: Seattle Proposition 1 — Property Tax Levy for Seattle Public Library

exclusions: Exclude districts that do not intersect Seattle.
Exclude County Council District 4 because the official primary files contain no contest for it.
Exclude the Northeast District Court contest because that electoral district does not intersect Seattle.
Do not derive the ballot universe from endorsement publications.
selection_method.rule: Start with King County Elections' official primary candidate, PCO, and composite sample ballot files. Include statewide and countywide contests; exact candidate-file jurisdictions whose official current district maps intersect Seattle; City of Seattle contests and measures; and PCO contests whose official precinct name begins with "SEA ".
selection_method.target: City of Seattle August 4, 2026 primary ballots
